docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Smash Soda is an unofficial open source application for hosting rooms on Parsec, developed by the Soda Arcade team. It is not affiliated with Parsec.

Smash Soda gives greater control over your Parsec session, allowing you to chat with your guests, manage gamepads and more. You can also advertise your room publically with Soda Arcade.

Smash Soda uses a now retired SDK that Parsec used to provide to developers. Due to the SDK's terms, Smash Soda can no longer be distributed as a binary, and must be built from source.

Be wary downloading Smash Soda builds, as it's open source nature makes it easy for people to smuggle malicious code. You should always build it from source from the official repository ( https://github.com/soda-arcade/smash-soda ) or using the installer script (https://github.com/soda-arcade/smash-soda/releases/latest/installer.bat).

If you like Smash Soda and Soda Arcade, consider donating to help fund development!

docs.title: Development and Contributing
docs.text: This page focuses on contributor workflow. If you need setup/build instructions first, use Manual Build (Windows).

docs.title: Work on Smash Soda in VS Code
docs.text:
This repository includes shared VS Code task/launch profiles in:

  • .vscode/tasks.json
  • .vscode/launch.json

docs.title: Build in VS Code
docs.text:
• Press Ctrl+Shift+B to run the default build task (cmake-build-release).
  • Or run specific tasks from Terminal -> Run Task:
  • cmake-build-release
  • cmake-build-debug (builds RelWithDebInfo)

docs.title: Run or Debug in VS Code
docs.text:
Open Run and Debug and select:

  • SmashSoda Debug (CMake) for step debugging (F5)
  • SmashSoda Release (CMake) for release run (Ctrl+F5)

Both profiles launch from the executable output folder so runtime assets like fonts/, icons/, and sfx/ load correctly.

docs.title: Contributing with Pull Requests
docs.text:
Before coding:

  1. Check existing issues, or open one describing the change you want to make.
  2. Fork the repo to your GitHub account.
  3. Create a focused feature branch.
git checkout -b feat/short-description

While working:

  1. Keep changes scoped to one feature/fix.
  2. Build at least:
  3. Release
  4. RelWithDebInfo
  5. Update docs if behavior/setup changed.

Submit your PR:

  1. Commit your branch.
  2. Push to your fork.
  3. Open a pull request to soda-arcade/smash-soda.
  4. Include:
  5. What changed
  6. Why it changed
  7. Any issue link(s)
  8. Screenshots/video for UI changes when useful

After opening:

  1. Respond to review feedback.
  2. Push follow-up commits to the same branch until approved.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Smash Soda must be built from source due to licensing issues.

docs.title: Installer
docs.text:
The easiest way to do this is with the installer batch script at:

https://github.com/soda-arcade/smash-soda/releases/latest/installer.bat

Download this, then right click it and select "Run as administrator". The script will take you through a guided setup.

docs.title: Manual build (if installer fails)
docs.text:
If the installer does not work on your machine, follow the manual guide:

Manual Build (Windows)

docs.title: Manual Build (Windows)
docs.text: If the installer script does not work for your system, you can build Smash Soda manually.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Git for Windows
  • CMake (3.18 or newer), added to PATH
  • Visual Studio Build Tools with:
  • Desktop development with C++
  • MSVC C++ toolset
  • Windows 10/11 SDK

docs.title: Migration
docs.text: Several things have been changed from the old version of Smash Soda (Legacy) to the new version. This page outlines the changes.

docs.title: Changes
docs.text:
• Smash Soda's configuration files have been moved from "C:\Users{USERNAME}\AppData\Roaming\SmashSodaTwo" to "C:\Users{USERNAME}\AppData\Roaming\Trybuchet\Smash Soda".

  • The Smash Soda overlay application has been renamed to "Smash Glass", and no longer needs to be added manually. Smash Soda will automatically download it.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Smash Soda interface is broken up in to widgets, which can be toggled and moved around. A widget can be moved by left click and dragging it by it's title bar. A widget can be resized by left click and dragging one of it's corners.

Analytical information about Smash Soda can be found in the bottom left corner.

Parsec and Soda Arcade account information can be found in the bottom right corner.

The vertical nav bar in the top left corner allows you to toggle the visibilty of widgets (hover over an icon to see which widget it corresponds to).
